title: Local file context integration
description: File picker via Tauri dialog plugin. Read file contents. Context card rendering with filename, first few lines preview, relative size bar. Card expand/reorder/remove. Optional watch mode via notify crate. Split from mood-editor-ona.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: File card component renders filename, preview, size bar correctly. Card reorder updates sort_order.
- Integration: Pick file via dialog mock → verify content read and cached. Watch file → modify → verify cache update event fires.
- Manual: File picker opens native dialog. Card preview shows useful first lines. Size bar proportions make sense. Expand/collapse is smooth.

title: Conversation forking
description: Cmd+B conversation forking via pi fork(). Branch comparison.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: Fork point detection from selected message. Pi session.fork() creates correct JSONL branch.
- Integration: Fork at message 3 of 5 → verify new session shares first 3 messages. Both branches independently continuable. Fork appears as linked note in library.
- Manual: Cmd+B on a message feels intuitive. Fork comparison side-by-side is readable. Navigating between forks is smooth.

- Manual: OAuth flow completes without confusion. Page browser shows workspace hierarchy. Converted content is accurate.

title: Wayland/WebKitGTK slow first paint and blank screen workaround
description: First launch on Wayland+WebKitGTK can show blank white screen due to slow initial load. May also need WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 on some GPU/driver combos. Add env var to dev script and document workaround. Also investigate adding a loading splash or spinner.

title: Inline AI document generation
description: Connect Tab-to-invoke UX to pi agent. Stream AI-generated text in-place at insertion point. Generated text appears slightly lighter. Press Enter to accept (text normalizes). Press Esc to dismiss generated text. Split from mood-editor-smr.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: Generation state machine (idle → generating → preview → accepted/dismissed). Content insertion at correct cursor position.
- Integration: Submit instruction → mock pi response → verify text streams into document at insertion point. Accept normalizes text style. Dismiss removes generated text.
- Manual: Generated text appears slightly lighter (distinguishable). Streaming in-place feels natural. Accept/dismiss is instant.

acceptance_criteria:

- Integration: IPC round-trip — invoke create_session from frontend mock, verify event stream delivers to JS
- Manual: Send a real message with valid API key, verify streaming response renders token-by-token without jank

title: Wire conversation and editor views into Page surface

title: Library search and filtering

acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): Search input debounces correctly. Tag qualifier parsing (tag:plan, status:active) extracts correct filter params.
- Integration: Create tagged notes, search by FTS term, verify results. Filter by tag, verify only matching notes show. Status filter hides completed by default.
- Manual: Search feels instant (<100ms perceived). Tag click filters immediately. Clearing search restores full list without flicker.

title: Distill flow (conversation to document)

acceptance_criteria:

- Integration: Conversation with 5 messages → Cmd+D → type 'plan' → verify new document note created with distilled_from link. Verify distilled content is structured per template.
- Manual: Distill prompt appears inline naturally. AI synthesis produces coherent output. Linked pair navigable from library.

## Tests

- Integration: Create notes via IPC, open library panel, verify list matches DB state. Verify Cmd+O toggles panel.
- Manual: Slide-in animation is 150-200ms, smooth. Note excerpts are readable. Date formatting is human-friendly. Panel doesn't occlude content awkwardly.

title: Tab-to-invoke inline AI UX

## Tests

- Manual: Glowing insertion line is visible but not distracting. Typing instruction feels natural. Esc dismisses cleanly. Tab elsewhere (mid-line) does nothing unusual.

title: Fix conversation forking to use pi-native session fork
description:
Current implementation stringifies conversation history into append_system_prompt for a new session. This destroys conversation structure (model sees flat text not turns), loses JSONL DAG continuity, has no parent_session linking, and cannot switch back to the original branch.

Should use pi's native fork: add get_fork_messages and fork commands to the PiBridge that mirror the RPC handler logic — plan_fork_from_user_message on the Session, create new JSONL with shared entries, swap the active session in-place. The SQLite note linking (forked_from) is fine, but the pi session manipulation must go through the SDK.

Revert the app-level fork implementation (commit 703ad38) and replace with native pi fork.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Bridge fork command creates new JSONL with shared entries up to fork point. get_fork_messages returns forkable entry IDs.
- Integration: Fork at message 3 of 5 → new session shares first 3 entries via JSONL DAG, not string duplication. Both branches continuable. parent_session set correctly.
- Manual: Ctrl+B forks, old branch accessible via library, new branch continues from fork point.

title: Fix context injection to use pi extensions instead of message appending
description:
Current implementation appends assembled context set content to the user's message string in useConversation (messageWithContext = trimmed + contextStr). The LLM sees context as part of every user message.

Problems:
- Bloats every user message with repeated context (context re-sent on every turn)
- Makes context invisible to pi's session history (buried in message text)
- Breaks on re-send/edit — context duplicated or lost
- Wastes tokens since context is repeated in each message, not in system prompt

The plan explicitly specifies context sets should be injected via pi extensions (enable_extensions_with_policy) or at minimum via append_system_prompt on the session. Pi extensions can inject system-level context that persists across turns without polluting user messages.

Fix: Remove message-appending from useConversation. Set append_system_prompt when creating/connecting the session with assembled context, or implement a pi extension that reads from SQLite. Update when context items change (may require session reconnect or a set_system_prompt bridge command).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Context content is in system prompt, not in user message text.
- Integration: Send 3 messages with context set active → verify context appears once in system prompt, not 3x in user messages.
- Manual: Context tray items influence AI responses without appearing in conversation message bubbles.

title: Claude conversation import
description: Parse Claude JSON export into pi JSONL sessions + SQLite note records.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: JSON parser handles Claude export format. Message structure preserved (user/assistant alternation). Timestamps mapped correctly. Malformed JSON returns clear error.
- Integration: Import real Claude export file → verify note count matches conversations. Verify each note has pi_session JSONL. Verify #imported/claude tag. Verify FTS indexes imported content.
- Manual: Import completes in reasonable time for 100+ conversations. Imported conversations are browsable and continuable.

title: Fix distill flow to actually run AI synthesis instead of saving raw prompt
description:
Current implementation builds a synthesis prompt with buildDistillPrompt() and saves it as the new note's content. It never sends it to the AI. The user opens a note that literally contains 'Distill the following conversation into a plan document...' with the raw conversation pasted in.

Should: Create a pi session, send the distill prompt, stream the AI's synthesized output, and save the AI's response as the document content. The distill flow should show a streaming indicator while generating, then save the final output.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Distill sends prompt to pi session and accumulates AI response as note content.
- Integration: Distill a 5-message conversation → new note contains AI-synthesized document, not the raw prompt template.
- Manual: Cmd+D shows streaming indicator, resulting document is well-structured AI output with proper formatting.

title: Tag-based mode switching and templates

acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit: Tag parser extracts #chat/#plan/#blog/#tweet from first line. Mode resolver returns correct Enter behavior per tag. Template loader returns correct template per tag.
- Integration: Type #plan as first word in new note → verify template loads and Enter inserts newline. Type #chat → verify Enter sends message.
- Manual: Mode switch feels instant. Template content is useful. No visual jarring on mode transition.

## Tests

title: Conversation message rendering and layout

acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): Message component renders user vs AI messages with correct styling classes. Empty conversation shows placeholder.
- Integration: Load a pi JSONL session file, verify all messages render in correct order with correct roles.
- Manual: Verify visual weight distinction between user and AI text is readable. Scroll behavior feels natural with long conversations.

title: Conversation rendering and message flow
description: Subscribe to pi events. Render conversation messages on page. Enter-to-send.

title: Hydrate conversation messages from pi session instead of React-only state
description:
Current implementation maintains conversation messages entirely in React state (useState in useConversation), accumulated from streaming events. It never reads from pi's session. Problems:

- On page reload or note switch, messages are lost (pi has them in JSONL, but the hook doesn't hydrate from there)
- Fork/branch operations depend on React state, not the authoritative pi session state
- Conversation as displayed may drift from what pi actually has (after compaction, steering, aborts)
- Loading an existing conversation note shows empty messages even though the JSONL has full history

The authoritative message list should come from handle.messages() (pi's to_messages_for_current_path()). React state can cache for rendering performance, but should hydrate from pi session on note load and reconcile after operations like fork/compact.

Requires: Add a get_messages bridge command that returns the current session's message list. useConversation hydrates from this on connect/reconnect. Streaming events still update incrementally for real-time display.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unit: Loading an existing conversation note hydrates messages from pi session JSONL.
- Integration: Create conversation with 5 messages → switch to another note → switch back → all 5 messages visible (hydrated from session, not lost).
- Manual: Reopening an old conversation shows full history. Messages survive page reload.

title: Hybrid inline Markdown rendering with cursor-aware toggle
description: CodeMirror 6 extension for hybrid inline rendering: headings render as headings, bold as bold, code blocks with syntax highlighting. When cursor enters a Markdown element, raw syntax appears. When cursor leaves, it re-renders. Progressive enhancement on top of basic CM6 setup. Split from mood-editor-2y0.
acceptance_criteria:
## Tests
- Unit (Vitest): CM6 extension produces correct decorations for headings, bold, code blocks. Cursor enter/leave toggles raw syntax.
- Manual: Open editor, type markdown with h1/h2/bold/code/links. Move cursor through each element — verify raw syntax appears at cursor, renders away when cursor leaves. Check no flicker on fast cursor movement.
